items.title: 'Girl who screams most nights': NT hearings address child protection system

children.content: Some foster carers have voiced support for the NT's proposed changes to child protection laws, but others say the current legislation already outlines individual safety as the paramount consideration.

image.altText: Nine people sitting along a long table, each with microphones before them, facing another table where politicians are sitting.

items.title: Remote community love story told through first-time director's rom-com

children.content: In a First Nations romantic comedy debuting in Sydney this week, a new writer-director has used her lived experience to tell the story of young love in a remote Northern Territory community.

image.altText: A young Indigenous woman looks over her left shoulder towards a shirtless man standing behind her.

items.title: Couple back at Finke Desert Race after finding love via social post

children.content: When motorbike enthusiast Ben Rawcliffe posted on a Finke Desert Race Facebook group looking for his future wife, his post garnered hundreds of comments. Now, he's back in the red centre, supporting his new partner as she takes to the tough off-road track.

articles.title: Finke Desert Race legends reflect on event ahead of 50th anniversary

children.content: The 460-kilometre test of skill and endurance through the central Australian outback has become one of the most famous off-road races in the world.

articles.title: Hollywood legend set to bring Finke Desert Race to the big screen

children.content: The gruelling outback Finke Desert Race is set for Hollywood treatment after Marvel's Chris Hemsworth backs a feature-length movie.

image.altText: Chris Hemsworth stares into the camera.

gallery.altText: A man and a woman in motorbike fear in front of a range with the sun behind.

thumbnail.altText: A man and a woman in motorbike fear in front of a range with the sun behind.

items.title: Country's biggest barra fishing comp under way, with some reporting more crocs

children.content: The country's biggest annual barramundi fishing competition is under way on the Northern Territory's remote Daly River, after the tournament was delayed by six weeks due to extensive flooding.

image.altText: White man standing on boat, wearing long sleeve purple / navy hoodie, holding barramundi fish from belly and mouth, posing

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family were forced to leave home community, MP says

children.content: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour says the mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby had been living in insecure housing in Alice Springs so she could access specialist support for her daughter, before the five-year-old's tragic death in April.

image.altText: Two women sit opposite each other.

items.title: 'Like opening an oven': NT constable praised for 'heroic' rescue attempt

children.content: A "heroic" Alice Springs police officer who risked his life repeatedly entering a burning building in trying to rescue a three-year-old boy will be awarded a medal of valour.

image.altText: A shirt memorialising Mitchell Thomas that reads In loving memory of Mitchell

items.title: Daly River service providers facing massive clean-up bill after record flood

children.content: Residents of the Northern Territory town of Nauiyu / Daly River have started returning home after spending three months in evacuation centres following extensive flooding.

image.altText: A man in a bright orange shirt sprays the muddy blue wall of a house

items.title: Video shows man escaping court custody in Darwin, triggering police manhunt

children.content: A manhunt is under way in Darwin as police search for Brandt Graham, a 44-year-old who allegedly scaled an enclosed courthouse dock and escaped from custody today.

image.altText: The outside of the ABC Darwin building, looking out towards the Darwin Local Court.

gallery.altText: A man in a green top running across a road, away from a court building, in a CBD.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a green top running across a road, away from a court building, in a CBD.

items.title: NT Supreme Court clears way for potential wave of summary adjournments

children.content: The Northern Territory Supreme Court has cleared the way for criminal defendants refused legal aid on funding grounds to have their cases adjourned indefinitely by overturning its own two-decade old ruling.

image.altText: a woman wearing glasses and a paisley dress sitting in a dark room

items.title: Cost-of-living pressure 'entrenching disadvantage' in the NT

children.content: The NT has the second highest average rent in the country, with households having to set aside more of their weekly wages to cover essential costs.

image.altText: Aerial shot of houses in Darwin

items.title: What's driving Australia's lentil boom?

children.content: Australia's winter crop production is expected to be down this year, but lentils are bucking the trend and headed towards a record.

gallery.altText: Three glass jars. One filled with red lentils, one with yellow lentils and the third with barley.
Woman assembles two lentil bowls from trays of baked lentils and sweet potato rounds. Coriander leaves, red onion and a dress

thumbnail.altText: Three glass jars. One filled with red lentils, one with yellow lentils and the third with barley.
Woman assembles two lentil bowls from trays of baked lentils and sweet potato rounds. Coriander leaves, red onion and a dress

items.title: US and Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The US military says it has shot down four drones Iran has launched toward the Strait of Hormuz and struck some of Tehran's coastal surveillance radar sites in response.

image.altText: A group of people paddle in water.

items.title: New AUKUS risk as Trump orders 'Golden Fleet' battleships

children.content: A committee of Congress wants the US Navy to explain how building "Trump-class" battleships will not delay other nuclear-powered boats, such as submarines needed for AUKUS.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands next to a posterboard showing a "Trump-class" submarine

items.title: How Everest guide survived six days alone with no oxygen and a few chocolates

children.content: A few chocolates found in his pocket and an avalanche may have been what saved Dawa Sherpa from almost certain death on Mount Everest.

image.altText: Dawa Sherpa being rescued.

gallery.altText: A Nepali man in a blue snow suit

thumbnail.altText: A Nepali man in a blue snow suit

items.title: Suns and Lions locked in heated QClash, Dockers claim record win

children.content: Fremantle beats North Melbourne by 124 points to claim the biggest win in its history, before the Suns try to put another dent in the Lions' flag hopes in the QClash. Follow live.

image.altText: Will Ashcroft and Lachie Neale both try to win the ball under pressure from Gold Coast opponents

items.title: Socceroos star labels US punditry 'rubbish' ahead of FIFA World Cup

children.content: Connor Metcalfe takes aim at comments coming from US experts ahead of the Socceroos' upcoming FIFA World Cup campaign.

image.altText: A soccer player in yellow has the ball next to a player in white

items.title: Karen Read sues police for alleged misconduct over boyfriend's death

children.content: After being cleared of allegations she hit her police officer boyfriend and left him to die, Karen Read now claims agencies that investigated the incident were riddled with "bigotry, misogyny [and] systemic failures".

image.altText: Karen Read in a sky blue pant suit speaking while exasperated in front of multiple black microphones, surrounded by men.

items.title: Woman charged with hindering police investigation after man dies at Tweed Heads

children.content: Police have arrested a man and charged a woman after a man died following an alleged assault on the New South Wales North Coast.

image.altText: A suburban street with police tape and a police van in the foreground

items.title: Inflatable frogs and chicken suits: How protests are approaching Trump 2.0

children.content: Demonstrators are increasingly employing inflatable costumes and whimsy. But behind the dress up, these protesters argue it is "serious play".

gallery.altText: A person in leopard an pink leggings dances.
An inflatfrog stands next to armed troops.

thumbnail.altText: A person in leopard an pink leggings dances.
An inflatfrog stands next to armed troops.

items.title: The club I wanted to join was waiting in Pompeii

children.content: A newly uncovered fresco in Pompeii tells the story of women, wine and Roman hedonism. Buried beneath the spectacle lies a lesson about restraint and why some discoveries should be left to the future.

image.altText: painted murals on a wall in an excavated building in Pompeii

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Mother creates modest activewear to empower herself and others

children.content: Yasmin Jammoul was sick of wearing oversized men's T-shirts to the gym when she started exercising postpartum, so she created her own clothing line.

image.altText: A woman with a dark green hijab and black outfit sitting on a chair smiling.

items.title: The US ally getting nuclear-powered submarines with no AUKUS deal

children.content: South Korea is taking a different path to Australia in building nuclear-powered submarines, but its plan still hinges on the United States.

image.altText: Three men in white uniforms and caps salute at the entrance on top of a large grey submarine, with a South Korean flag behind.

items.title: Anthony Albanese has shown he understands the Westminster system

children.content: This week the Coalition's weakness in Question Time reminded onlookers of Albanese's command of the Westminster system, while Labor insiders expressed their anxieties about One Nation's polling surge.

image.altText: Albanese looking down in a suit while touching his ear.

items.title: Fastest-rising women's cancer in Australia still has a low profile

children.content: The incidence of uterine cancer has doubled over the past 25 years. But it's also a disease without a public profile, which means many women aren't aware of the warning signs or the risk factors.

gallery.altText: A composite image of a baby in a trolley next to a portrait image of a woman smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of a baby in a trolley next to a portrait image of a woman smiling.

items.title: Nightmare turnover leaves Wembanyama, Spurs in NBA Finals hole

children.content: Victor Wembanyama turns the ball over and misses two shots in a horror final minute as the Knicks take a 2-0 series lead back to New York.

image.altText: Victor Wembanyama reacts after missing a shot against Knicks

items.title: Korda stages comeback as four Aussies advance at US Open

children.content: World No.1 Nelly Korda edges closer to the top of the leaderboard at Riviera as Grace Kim leads the way for the Australian contingent.

image.altText: Nelly Korda leans on her golf club and watches a putt travel

items.title: The fake police scam stealing millions from Australia's Chinese community

children.content: Justin thought he was being investigated by the Chinese police, but was pulled into a vicious and frightening scam targeting the Chinese diaspora all over the world.

image.altText: A man looks worried as he speaks to someone over the phone.

items.title: Anthropic calls for AI freeze with fears humans risk 'losing control'

children.content: As a major AI company warns of an increased risk of "humans losing control over AI systems", the Trump administration says it will accelerate the development and use of artificial intelligence for national security applications.

image.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

items.title: Residents say public housing works have blocked them from their own yards

children.content: Neighbours of a Homes Tasmania development in Burnie say excavation works have caused their land to start slipping downhill, restricting access to parts of their property.

image.altText: A man with grey hair and casual navy clothes stands in front of a construction site.

items.title: Lorna has lived through two world wars, two pandemics and 26 Australian prime ministers

children.content: After a couple of years as the country's oldest woman, Lorna Henstridge is still going strong.

image.altText: An elderly woman holding an oval framed photo of a young child wearing Elizabethan costume

items.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds

children.content: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

gallery.altText: Andrew seen through the window of a car, with a red, purplish mark on his cheek.
A close up of Prince Andrew looking over his shoulder and wearing a suit.

thumbnail.altText: Andrew seen through the window of a car, with a red, purplish mark on his cheek.
A close up of Prince Andrew looking over his shoulder and wearing a suit.

items.title: Hawks forward target of racist online abuse after loss to Bulldogs

children.content: The AFL and Hawthorn come out in support of Mabior Chol after the Hawks forward shares a series of abusive messages he received after Friday night's match against the Bulldogs.

image.altText: Mabior Chol takes a mark for the Hawks during an AFL match.

items.title: Meet the Filipinas inspiring a new motorsport generation

children.content: Julia de los Angeles loves being at the top of the Philippine motorsport scene. She sheds light on what it's like to be one of the few women among mostly men.

image.altText: A woman sits in a racing car with her helmet on.

items.title: Acid attack victim's lawyer describes trial of perpetrators as a 'sham'

children.content: Four military officers are facing trial over an acid attack on an Indonesian human rights campaigner that left him seriously disfigured. But some lawyers say the legal process is a "sham".

avatar.alt: Tim Swanston wearing a white shirt stands in front of a camera.
